author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XVI. 24.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus unto His disciples, If any man will come after me, let him renounce himself,and take up his cross and follow me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When Peter said, “Be it far from Thee, this shall not be unto Thee;” and was told, “Get thee behind me, Satan.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He was by no means satisfied with the mere rebuke, but, willing also more abundantly to show both the extravagance of what Peter had said, and the benefit of His passion, He saith, “Thy word to me is, “Be it far from Thee, this shall not be unto Thee:” but my word to thee is, “Not only is it hurtful to thee, and destructive, to hinder me and to be displeased at my Passion, but it will be impossible for thee even to be saved, unless thou thyself too be continually prepared for death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, lest they should think His suffering unworthy of Him, not by the former things only, but also by the events that were coming on, He teaches them the gain thereof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus in John first, He saith, “Except the corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone; but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit;”but here more abundantly working it out, not concerning Himself only doth He bring forward the statement that it is meet to die, but concerning them also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For so great is the profit thereof, that in your case also unwillingness to die is grievous, but to be ready for it, good.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This however He makes clear by what follows, but for the present He works it out on one side only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how He also makes His discourse unexceptionable: not saying at all, “whether you will, or no, you must suffer this,” but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “If any man will come after me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “I force not, I compel not, but each one I make lord of his own choice; wherefore also I say, ‘If any man will.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to good things do I call you, not to things evil, or burdensome; not to punishment and vengeance, that I should have to compel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, the nature of the thing is alone sufficient to attract you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now, thus saying, He drew them unto Him the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he indeed that uses compulsion oftens turns men away, but he that leaves the hearer to choose attracts him more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For soothing is a mightier thing than force.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore even He Himself said, “If any man will.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For great,” saith He, “are the good things which I give you, and such as for men even to run to them of their own accord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither if one were giving gold, and offering a treasure, would he invite with force.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if that invitation be without compulsion, much more this, to the good things in the Heavens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if the nature of the thing persuade thee not to run, thou art not worthy to receive it at all, nor if thou shouldest receive it, wilt thou well know what thou hast received.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ compels not, but urges, sparing us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since they seemed to be murmuring much, being secretly disturbed at the saying, He saith, “No need of disturbance or of trouble.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If ye do not account what I have mentioned to be a cause of innumerable blessings, even when befalling yourselves, I use no force, nor do I compel, but if any be willing to follow, him I call.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For do not by any means imagine that this is your following of me; I mean, what ye now do attending upon me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye have need of many toils, many dangers, if ye are to come after me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou oughtest not, O Peter, because thou hast confessed me Son of God, therefore only to expect crowns, and to suppose this enough for thy salvation, and for the future to enjoy security, as having done all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For although it be in my power, as Son of God, to hinder thee from having any trial at all of those hardships; yet such is not my will, for thy sake, that thou mayest thyself too contribute something, and be more approved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so, if one were a judge at the games, and had a friend in the lists, he would not wish to crown him by favor only, but also for his own toils; and for this reason especially, because he loves him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so Christ also; whom He most loves, those He most of all will have to approve themselves by their own means also, and not from His help alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see how at the same time He makes His saying not a grievous one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He doth by no means compass them only with His terror, but He also puts forth the doctrine generally to the world, saying, “If any one will,” be it woman or man, ruler or subject, let him come this way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And though he seem to have spoken but one single thing, yet His sayings are three, “Let him renounce himself,” and “Let him bear his cross,” and “Let him follow me;” and two of them are joined together, but the one is put by itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us see first what it can be to deny one’s self.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us learn first what it is to deny another, and then we shall know what it may be to deny one’s self.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is it to deny another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that is denying another,—for example, either brother, or servant, or whom you will,—should he see him either beaten, or bound, or led to execution, or whatever he may suffer, stands not by him, doth not help him, is not moved, feels nothing for him, as being once for all alienated from him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus then He will have us disregard our own body, so that whether men scourge, or banish, or burn, or whatever they do, we may not spare it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is to spare it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since fathers too then spare their offspring, when committing them to teachers, they command not to spare them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So also Christ; He said not, “Let him not spare himself,” but very strictly, “Let him renounce himself;” that is, let him have nothing to do with himself, but give himself up to all dangers and conflicts; and let him so feel, as though another were suffering it all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not, “Let him deny,”but “Let him renounce;”even by this small addition intimating again, how very far it goes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this latter is more than the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And let him take up his cross.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This arises out of the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to hinder thy supposing that words, and insults, and reproaches are to be the limits of our self-renunciation, He saith also how far one ought to renounce one’s self; that is, unto death, and that a reproachful death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said not, “Let him renounce himself unto death,” but, “Let him take up his cross;” setting forth the reproachful death; and that not once, nor twice, but throughout all life one ought so to do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Yea,” saith He, “bear about this death continually, and day by day be ready for slaughter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since many have indeed contemned riches, and pleasure, and glory, but death they despised not, but feared dangers; I,” saith He, “will that my champion should wrestle even unto blood, and that the limits of his course should reach unto slaughter; so that although one must undergo death, death with reproach, the accursed death, and that upon evil surmise, we are to bear all things nobly, and rather to rejoice in being suspected.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And let him follow me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, it being possible for one to suffer, yet not to follow Him, when one doth not suffer for Him (for so robbers often suffer grievously, and violaters of tombs, and sorcerers); to hinder thy supposing that the mere nature of thy calamities is sufficient, He adds the occasion of these calamities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order that, so doing and suffering, thou mayest follow Him; that for Him thou mayest undergo all things; that thou mayest possess the other virtues also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this too is expressed by “Let him follow me;” so as to show forth not fortitude only, such as is exercised in our calamities, but temperance also, and moderation, and all self-restraint.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This being properly “to follow,” the giving heed also to the other virtues, and for His sake suffering all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there are who follow the devil even to the endurance of all this, and for his sake give up their own lives; but we for Christ, or rather for our own sakes: they indeed to harm themselves both here and there; but we, that we may gain both lives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then is it not extreme dullness, not to show forth even the same fortitude with them that perish; and this, when we are to reap from it so many crowns?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet with us surely Christ Himself is present to be our help, but with them no one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now He had indeed already spoken this very injunction, when He sent them, saying, “Go not into the way of the Gentiles” (for, saith He, “I send you as sheep in the midst of wolves,” and, “ye shall be brought before kings and governors”)but now with more intensity and severity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then He spake of death only, but here He hath mentioned a cross also, and a continual cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “let him take up,” saith He, “his cross;” that is, “let him carry it continually and bear it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He is wont to do in everything; not in the first instance, nor from the beginning, but quietly and gradually, bringing in the greater commandments, that the hearers may not count it strange.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, because the saying seemed to be vehement, see how He softens it by what follows, and sets down rewards surpassing our toils; and not rewards only, but also the penalties of vice: nay, on these last He dwells more than on those, since not so much His bestowing blessings, as His threat of severities, is wont to bring ordinary men to their senses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at least how He both begins here from this, and ends in this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For whosoever will save his life shall lose it,” saith He, “but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake, shall find it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what is a man profited,if he should gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now what He saith is like this: “not as unsparing towards you, but rather as exceedingly sparing you, I enjoin these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he who spares his child, ruins it; but he who spares it not, preserves.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To which effect also a certain wise man said, “If thou beat thy son with a rod, he shall not die, but thou shalt deliver his soul from death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “He that refresheth his son, shall bind up his wounds.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This takes place in the camp also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the general, sparing the soldiers, commands them to remain within the place always, he will destroy with them the inhabitants too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “In order then that this may not happen in your case also,” saith He, “ye must be arrayed against continual death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For now too a grievous war is about to be kindled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Sit not therefore within, but go forth and fight; and shouldest thou fall in thy post, then hast thou obtained life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if in the visible wars he that in his post meets slaughter, is both more distinguished than the rest, and more invincible, and more formidable to the enemy; although we know that after death the king, in behalf of whom he takes his station, is not able to raise him up again: much more in these wars, when there are such hopes of resurrection besides, will he who exposes his own life unto death, find it; in one sense, because he will not be quickly taken; in a second, because even though he fall, Godwill lead his life on to a higher life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, because he had said, “He who will save shall lose it, but whosoever shall lose shall save it,” and on that side had set salvation and destruction, and on this salva tion and destruction; to prevent any one’s imagining the one destruction and salvation to be all the same with the other, and to teach thee plainly that the difference between this salvation and that is as great as between destruction and salvation; from the contraries also He makes an inference once for all to establish these points.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For what is a man profited,”saith He, “if he gain the whole world, and lose his own soul ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how the wrongful preservation of it is destruction, and worse than all destruction, as being even past remedy, from the want of anything more to redeem it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “tell me not this,” saith He, “that he that hath escaped such dangers hath saved his life; but together with his life put also the whole world, yet what profit hath he thereby, if the soul perish?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, shouldest thou see thy servants in luxury, and thyself in extreme calamity, wilt thou indeed profit aught by being master?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Make this reckoning then with regard to thy soul also, when the flesh is in luxury and wealth, and she awaiting the destruction to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “What shall a man give in exchange for his soul ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, He dwells upon the same point.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? hast thou another soul to give for this soul? saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, shouldest thou lose money, thou wilt be able to give money; or be it house, or slaves, or any other kind of possession, but for thy soul, if thou lose it, thou wilt have no other soul to give: yea, though thou hadst the world, though thou wast king of the whole earth, thou wouldest not be able, by paying down all earthly goods, with the earth itself, to redeem but one soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what marvel, if it be so with the soul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since even in the body one may see that so it turns out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though thou wear ten thousand diadems, but have a body sickly by nature, and incurable, thou wilt not be able, not by giving all thy kingdom, to recover this body, not though thou add innumerable persons, and cities, and goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now thus I bid thee reason with regard to thy soul also; or rather even much more with regard to the soul; and do thou, forsaking all besides, spend all thy care upon it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not then while taking thought about the things of others, neglect thyself and thine own things; which now all men do, resembling them that work in the mines.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither do these receive any profit from this labor, nor from the wealth; but rather great harm, both because they incur fruitless peril, and incur it for other men, reaping no benefit from such their toils and deaths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These even now are objects of imitation to many, who are digging up wealth for others; or rather we are more wretched even than this, inasmuch as hell itself awaits us after these our labors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they indeed are staid from those toils by death, but to us death proves a beginning of innumerable evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou say, thou hast in thy wealth the fruit of thy toils: show me thy soul gladdened, and then I am persuaded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of all things in us the soul is chief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if the body be fattened, while she is pining away, this prosperity is nothing to thee (even as when the handmaiden is glad, the happiness of the maidservant is nothing to her mistress perishing, nor is the fair robe anything compared with the weak flesh); but Christ will say unto thee again, “What shall a man give in exchange for his soul ?” on every hand commanding thee to be busied about that, and to take account of it only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having alarmed them therefore hereby, He comforts them also by His good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the Son of Man shall come,” saith He, “in the glory of His Father with His holy angels, and then He shall reward every man according to his works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how the glory of the Father and of the Son is all one?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if the glory be one, it is quite evident that the substance also is one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if in one substance there be a difference of glory (“for there is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for one star differeth from another star in glory;”although the substance be one), how may the substance of those differ, whereof the glory is one?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not at all, “In glory such as the Father’s,” whereby thou mightest suppose again some variation; but implying entire perfection, “In that same glory,” saith He, “will He come;” for it to be deemed one and the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now, why fear, O Peter” (so He speaks), “on being told of death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, then shalt thou see me in the glory of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if I am in glory, so are ye; your interests are no wise limited to the present life, but another sort of portion will take you up, a better one.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, when He had spoken of the good things, He stayed not at this, but mingled the fearful things also, bringing forward that judgment-seat, and the inexorable account, and the inflexible sentence, and the judgment that cannot be deceived.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He suffered not however His discourse to appear only dismal, but tempered it also with good hopes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did He say, “then shall He punish them that sinned,” but, “He shall reward every man according to his doings.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He said, reminding not only the sinners of punishment, but also them that have done well of prizes and crowns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He indeed spake it, in part to refresh the good, but I ever shudder at hearing it, for I am not of them that are crowned, and I suppose that others also share with us in our fear and anxiety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whom is this saying not enough to startle, when he hath entered into his own conscience; and to make him shudder, and convince him that we have need of sackcloth, and of prolonged fasting, more than the people of the Ninevites?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not for an overthrow of a city, and the common end, are we concerned, but for eternal punishment, and the fire that is never quenched.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also I praise and admire the monks that have occupied the desert places, as for the rest, so for this saying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they after having made their dinners, or rather after supper (for dinner they know not at any time, because they know that the present time is one of mourning and fasting); after supper then, in saying certain hymns of thanksgiving unto God, they make mention of this expression also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if ye would hear the very hymns themselves, that ye too may say them continually, I will rehearse to you the whole of that sacred song.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The words of it then stand as follows: “Blessed God, who feedest me from my youth up, who givest food to all flesh; fill our hearts with joy and gladness, that always having all sufficiency we may abound unto every good work in Christ Jesus our Lord; with whom be unto Thee glory, honor and might, with the Holy Spirit, forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Glory to Thee, O Lord, glory to Thee, O Holy One, glory to Thee, O King, that Thou hast given us meat to make us glad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fill us with the Holy Ghost, that we may be found well-pleasing before Thee, not being ashamed, when Thou renderest to every man according to his works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now this hymn is in all parts worthy of admiration, but especially the above ending of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, because meals and food are wont to dissipate and weigh down, they put this saying as a kind of bridle upon the soul, at the time of indulgence reminding it of the time of judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they have learnt what befell Israel through a costly table.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For my beloved,” saith He, “ate, and waxed fat, and kicked.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also Moses said, “When thou shalt have eaten and drunk and art full, remember the Lord thy God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after that feast, then they ventured on those acts of lawless daring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou therefore also look to it, lest something like it befall thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though thou sacrifice not to stone nor to gold, either sheep or bullocks, see lest to wrath thou sacrifice thine own soul, lest to whoredom or other like passions, thou sacrifice thine own salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea—on this account, you see, they being afraid of these downfalls, when they have enjoyed their meal, or rather fasting (for their meal is in fact fasting), remind themselves of the terrible judgment-seat, and of that day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if they who correct themselves both with fasting, and with nights spent on the ground, with watchings, and with sackcloth, and with ten thousand means, do yet require also this reminding, when will it be possible for us to live virtuously; who set forth tables loaded with innumerable wrecks, and do not so much as pray at all, neither in the beginning nor the end ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore to put an end to these shipwrecks, let us bring before us that hymn and unfold it all, that seeing the profit thereof, we too may chant it constantly over our table, and quell the rude motions of the belly, introducing both the manners and laws of those angels into our houses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For you ought indeed to go there and reap these fruits; but since ye are not willing, at least through our words, hear this spiritual melody, and let every one after his meal say these words, beginning thus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the apostolic law they straightway fulfill, that commands, “Whatsoever we do in word or in deed, that we do it in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, giving thanks to God and the Father by Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Next, the thanksgiving takes place not for that one day only, but for all their life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Who feedest me,” it is said, “from my youth up.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And a lesson of self-command is drawn thence, that when God feeds, we must not take thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if upon a king’s promising thee to furnish thy daily food out of his own stores, thou wouldest be of good hope for the future; much more, when God gives, and all things pour upon thee as out of fountains, shouldest thou be freed from all anxiety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and to this very intent they so speak, that they may persuade both themselves, and those that are made disciples by them, to put off all worldly care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, not to have thee suppose that for themselves only they offer up this thanksgiving, they further say, “Who givest food to all flesh,” giving thanks in behalf of all the world; and as fathers of the whole earth, so do they offer up their praises for all, and train themselves to a sincere brotherly love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not even possible they should hate them, in behalf of whom they thank God, that they are fed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou both charity introduced by their thanksgiving, and worldly care cast out, both by the preceding words, and by these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He feed all flesh, much more them that are devoted to him; if them that are entangled in worldly cares, much more them that are freed from the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To establish this, Christ Himself said, “How many sparrows do ye exceed in value?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said it, teaching them not to put their confidence in wealth and land and seeds; for it is not these that feed us, but the word of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hereby they stop the mouths, both of the Manichæans, and of them of Valentinus, and of all that are diseased in their way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For sure this Being is not evil, who sets his own stores before all, even before them that blaspheme Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then comes the petition: “Fill our hearts with joy and gladness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With what manner of joy then, doth it mean? the joy of this world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: God forbid: for had they meant this, they would not have occupied summits of mountains, and deserts, nor wrapt themselves in sackcloth; but that joy they mean, which hath nothing in common with this present life, the joy of angels, the joy above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they do not simply ask for it, but in great excess; for they say not, “give,” but, “fill,” and they say not “us,” but “our hearts.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is especially a heart’s joy; “For the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, because sin brought in sorrow, they request that through joy righteousness may be implanted in them, for no otherwise might joy be engendered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “That, always having all sufficiency, we may abound unto every good work.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how they fulfill that word of the gospel which saith, “Give us this day our daily bread,” and how they seek even this for spiritual ends.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For their phrase is, “That we may abound unto every good work.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They said not, “That we may do our duty only,” but “even more than what is enjoined,” for, “that we may abound,” means this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And while of God they seek sufficiency in things needful, themselves are willing to obey not in sufficiency only, but with much abundance, and in all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is the part of well-disposed servants, this of men strict in goodness, to abound always, and in all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then again reminding themselves of their own weakness, and that without the influence from above nothing noble can be done; having said, “that we may abound unto every good work,” they add, “in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom unto Thee be glory, honor, and might forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Amen;” framing this end like their commencement by a thread of thanksgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this again, they seem to begin afresh, but they are keeping to the same argument.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As Paul also in the beginning of an epistle, having closed with a doxology, where he says, “According to the will of our God and Father, to whom be glory forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Amen;”begins the subject again on which he was writing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again in another place when he had said, “They worshipped and served the creature more than the Creator, who is blessed forever: Amen;”he completed not his discourse, but begins again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore neither let us blame these our angels, as acting disorderly, for that having closed with a doxology they begin again the sacred hymns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they follow apostolical laws, beginning from a doxology, and ending therein, and after that end making a commencement again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore they say, “Glory be to Thee, O Lord; glory be to Thee, O Holy One; glory be to Thee, O King; that Thou hast given us food to make us glad.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since not for the greater things only, but also for the lesser, we ought to give thanks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they do give thanks for these also, putting to shame the heresy of the Manichæans, and of as many as affirm our present life to be evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For lest for their high self-command, and contempt of the belly, thou shouldest suspect them as abhorring the meat, like the heretics aforesaid, who choke themselvesto death; they by their prayer teach thee, that not from abhorrence of God’s creatures they abstain from most of them, but as exercising self-restraint.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how after thanksgiving for His past gifts, they are importunate also for the greater things, and dwell not upon the mat ters of this life, but mount above the heavens, and say, “Fill us with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not even possible to approve one’s self as one ought, not being filled with that grace; as there is no doing anything noble or great, without the benefit of Christ’s influences.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As therefore when they had said, “That we may abound unto every good work,” they added, “In Christ Jesus;” so here also they say, “Fill us with the Holy Ghost, that we may be found to have been well-pleasing before Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how for the things of this life they pray not, but give thanks only; but for the things of the Spirit, they both give thanks and pray.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “seek ye,” saith He, “the kingdom of heaven, and all these things shall be added unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark too another kind of severe goodness in them; their saying, namely, “That we may be found to have been well-pleasing in Thy sight, not being ashamed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “we care not,” say they, “for the shame that proceeds from the many, but whatever men may say of us, laughing, upbraiding, we do not so much as regard it; but our whole endeavor is not to be put to shame then.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in these expressions, they bring in also the river of fire, and the prizes, and the rewards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They said not, “that we be not punished,” but, “that we be not ashamed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is to us far more fearful than hell, to seem to have offended our Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since the more part and the grosser sort are not in fear of this, they add, “When Thou renderest to every man according to his works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how greatly these strangers and pilgrims have benefitted us, these citizens of the wilderness, or rather citizens of the Heavens?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas we are strangers to the Heavens, but citizens of the earth, these are just the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And after this hymn, being filled with much compunction, and with many and fervent tears, so they proceed to sleep, snatching just so much of it as a little to refresh themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, the nights they make days, spending them in thanksgivings and in the singing of psalms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not men only, but women also practise this self-denial, overcoming the weakness of their nature by the abundance of their zeal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us be abashed then at their earnestness, we who are men, let us cease to be fastened to the things present, to shadow, to dreams, to smoke.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the more part of our life is passed in insensibility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both the first period of our life is full of much folly, and that again which travels on to old age, makes all the feeling that is in us wither away, and small is the space between, that is able feelingly to enjoy pleasure; or rather, not even that hath a pure participation thereof, by reason of innumerable cares and toils, that harrass it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, I pray, let us seek the unmovable and eternal goods, and the life that never has old age.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even one dwelling in a city may imitate the self-denial of the monks; yea, one who has a wife, and is busied in a household, may pray, and fast, and learn compunction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since they also, who at the first were instructed by the apostles, though they dwelt in cities, yet showed forth the piety of the occupiers of the deserts: and others again who had to rule over workshops, as Priscilla and Aquila.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the prophets too, all had both wives and households, as Isaiah, as Ezekiel, as the great Moses, and received no hurt therefrom in regard of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These then let us also imitate, and continually offer thanksgiving to God, continually sing hymns to Him; let us give heed to temperance, and to all other virtues, and the self-denial that is practised in the deserts, let us bring into our cities; that we may appear both well-pleasing before God, and approved before men, and attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom and with whom be unto the Father, glory, honor, and might, together with the holy and life-giving Spirit, now and always and world without end.
